revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂積虫麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q22129332|qid=Q22129332|1=Hozumi no Oyu}}; {{ill|Q22129418|qid=Q22129418|1=Hozumi no Yamamori}}|father={{ill|Q11597083|qid=Q11597083|1=Hozumi no Momotari}}}}
'''Hozumi no Mushimaro''' (穂積 虫麻呂) was a figure from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=kabane|1=Kabane}} (hereditary title) changed from {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}} to {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}}, and he held the Rank of {{ill|Chikōshi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}'s reign ({{ill|684|en|684|qid=Q31128|lt=684|1=684}}), the {{ill|Eight Rank Titles|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank Titles|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} system was established, and members of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} were elevated from the Omi kabane to the Ason kabane<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 11月1日条</ref>. It is presumed that Mushimaro also received this promotion at that time.
In the 1st year of the {{ill|Shuchō|en|Shuchō|qid=Q777413|lt=Shuchō era|1=Shuchō}} ({{ill|686|en|686|qid=Q30619|lt=686|1=686}}), Mushimaro was dispatched to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|qid=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} along with {{ill|Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja|河内王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} and {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} to host the {{ill|Silla envoy|ja|新羅使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla envoy|qq=}}, Kim Chisō. At the time, Mushimaro held the Rank of Chikōshi<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年正月条</ref>. Later that year, in September, Mushimaro participated in the rites at Emperor Tenmu’s {{ill|mortuary court|ja|殯庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mortuary court|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he reported on matters related to the provincial governors<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年9月29日条</ref>.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Nihon Shoki (Volume II)*, {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632220}}, Kodansha, 1988. * Suzuki Junosuke, *Hozumi-Suzuki Genealogy: The 270-Year History of the Suzuki Clan in Kishu under Tokugawa Rule*, 1982. * {{ill|Hoga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoga Toshio|1=Toshio Hōga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452691}}, *Comprehensive Genealogies of Ancient Clans*, Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|穂積虫麻呂|version=96880265|comment=Imported full ja history}}
